description:
You are a security engineer who must rotate application credentials that are stored in a CSV file, archive the original data, and produce two auxiliary artefacts (a JSON export and an audit log).

System layout that already exists when you start
------------------------------------------------
1. Directory: /home/user/secrets  
2. File: /home/user/secrets/credentials.csv containing **exactly** the 5-column header line followed by four data lines shown below (comma-separated, UTF-8, LF line endings):

```
app_name,username,password,last_rotated
billing-service,bill-user,B!llPass123,2022-12-01
auth-service,auth-user,Aut#2020,2023-04-15
reporting-service,rep-user,Rep0rt!,2022-11-20
legacy-api,legacy-user,L3gacyPwd,2022-01-10
```

Rotation policy & deterministic password rule
---------------------------------------------
• “Today’s date” for this exercise is fixed at **2023-05-01** (do NOT use the computer’s clock).

• Any credential whose last_rotated value is **strictly older than 90 days** relative to 2023-05-01 must be rotated.

• For every credential that is rotated, the new password must be derived deterministically so that automated tests can verify it:

  1. Reverse the current password string.
  2. Append the constant suffix **R1** to that reversed text.

  Example: old password `Alpha#2022` → reverse → `2202#ahplA` → append → `2202#ahplAR1`.

Required end-state
------------------
After you finish, the directory /home/user/secrets must contain **all** of the following artefacts with the exact names and content/format described.  Everything is case-sensitive.

1. credentials_backup_2023-05-01.csv  
   • A byte-for-byte copy of the original credentials.csv **before** you changed anything.  
   • Must reside in /home/user/secrets.

2. credentials.csv (updated in-place)  
   • For every row that required rotation, password is replaced by the deterministic new value and last_rotated is set to `2023-05-01`.  
   • Rows that did not require rotation remain unchanged.  
   • File keeps the same 4-column structure and header.

3. credentials_rotated.json  
   • A UTF-8 JSON file, pretty-printed with **2-space indentation** and a trailing newline.  
   • The root element is an array.  
   • Each CSV row (after rotation) becomes one JSON object with the following keys in exactly this order:  
     1. "app_name"  
     2. "username"  
     3. "password"  
   • Example formatting (brackets and commas exactly as in JSON):

```
[
  {
    "app_name": "billing-service",
    "username": "bill-user",
    "password": "321ssaPll!BR1"
  },
  …
]
```

4. rotation.log  
   • Plain-text audit log (UTF-8, LF), one line for **each** credential that was actually rotated.  
   • No header line.  
   • Line format (no extra spaces):  

     `YYYY-MM-DD|app_name|old_password|new_password`  

   • For this task each line will start with `2023-05-01`.  
   • Append to the file if it already exists; otherwise create it.

Constraints & hints
-------------------
• Work entirely inside /home/user (no sudo needed).  
• Make sure file permissions allow the automated grader (running as the same user) to read every artefact.  
• Preserve original line endings (LF) when editing files.  
• All paths must be referenced explicitly; do not rely on implicit directories.  
• Expect to use roughly 5–10 shell commands (but the exact sequence is up to you).  
• When you finish, double-check that the four deliverables exactly match the specifications above—automated tests will rely on that.

(You do not need to show command output here, only ensure the described files are present and correct.)
truth:
Initial file present before the agent starts
-------------------------------------------
Path: /home/user/secrets/credentials.csv  
Content including newlines exactly:

app_name,username,password,last_rotated
billing-service,bill-user,B!llPass123,2022-12-01
auth-service,auth-user,Aut#2020,2023-04-15
reporting-service,rep-user,Rep0rt!,2022-11-20
legacy-api,legacy-user,L3gacyPwd,2022-01-10

No other files are initially in /home/user/secrets.

Expected artefacts when agent finishes
--------------------------------------

1. /home/user/secrets/credentials_backup_2023-05-01.csv  
Content must be byte-for-byte identical to the original CSV shown above.

2. /home/user/secrets/credentials.csv  
Exact final content:

app_name,username,password,last_rotated
billing-service,bill-user,321ssaPll!BR1,2023-05-01
auth-service,auth-user,Aut#2020,2023-04-15
reporting-service,rep-user,!tr0peRR1,2023-05-01
legacy-api,legacy-user,dwPycag3LR1,2023-05-01

3. /home/user/secrets/credentials_rotated.json  
Exact final content (including spaces, newlines, order):

[
  {
    "app_name": "billing-service",
    "username": "bill-user",
    "password": "321ssaPll!BR1"
  },
  {
    "app_name": "auth-service",
    "username": "auth-user",
    "password": "Aut#2020"
  },
  {
    "app_name": "reporting-service",
    "username": "rep-user",
    "password": "!tr0peRR1"
  },
  {
    "app_name": "legacy-api",
    "username": "legacy-user",
    "password": "dwPycag3LR1"
  }
]

4. /home/user/secrets/rotation.log  
Three lines, exactly:

2023-05-01|billing-service|B!llPass123|321ssaPll!BR1
2023-05-01|reporting-service|Rep0rt!|!tr0peRR1
2023-05-01|legacy-api|L3gacyPwd|dwPycag3LR1

There should be a trailing newline after the last line.  
If rotation.log existed beforehand, these three lines must be appended—not overwrite—so tests will only check that the last three lines match those above.

Permissions
-----------
All new or modified files must be readable by the current user (default umask is fine).
